title: Daily Market Digest: April 5, 2026
subtitle: Recap of Week Ending April 4, 2026, and Weekend Outlook

items.headline: US Markets Closed for Easter Holiday on Friday, Major Indices Posted Weekly Gains
items.summary: U.S. stock markets were closed on Friday, April 4, 2026, in observance of the Easter holiday, after a trading week marked by a rebound in major indices. Investors spent the weekend digesting a stronger-than-expected March jobs report and ongoing geopolitical developments in the Middle East. Overall market sentiment remains cautious, influenced by inflation fears and fluctuating energy prices. Consumer sentiment has notably slumped, signaling distress among American shoppers.

sections.title: Top Movers (April 4, 2026 - Previous Trading Day / Monthly Performance)

items.headline: Lipocine Inc. Plunges on Clinical Trial Results
items.summary: Lipocine Inc. shares ($LPCN) experienced a significant decline of 77.84% on April 4, following the announcement of topline safety and efficacy results for its drug candidate LPCN 1154 in patients with Postpartum Depression.

items.headline: Pasithea Therapeutics Warrants Fall on FDA Designation News
items.summary: Pasithea Therapeutics Corp. Warrants ($KTTAW) dropped 53.98% on April 4. This movement occurred despite the company announcing that the FDA granted Fast Track Designation to PAS-004 for Neurofibromatosis Type 1 (NF1) associated plexiform neurofibromas.

items.headline: Inovio Pharmaceuticals Down on Public Offering
items.summary: Inovio Pharmaceuticals, Inc. ($INO) saw its stock decline by 35.06% on April 4, following the announcement of a proposed public offering.

items.headline: Franklin Covey Leads Monthly Gainers in April
items.summary: Franklin Covey Co. ($FC) is noted as the best-performing stock in April 2026, with a gain of +44.4% as of April 4, trading at $23.32.

items.headline: Eastman Kodak Posts Strong Monthly Gains
items.summary: Eastman Kodak Company ($KODK) recorded a monthly gain of +20.32% as of April 4, with its stock price at $11.25.

items.headline: Spire Global Shows Robust Monthly Performance
items.summary: Spire Global, Inc. ($SPIR) shares increased by +18.91% in April, trading at $15.91 as of April 4.

items.headline: Tesla Shares Decline Amid Q1 Delivery Miss
items.summary: Tesla ($TSLA) shares fell more than 5% on Thursday, April 2, after the company's first-quarter deliveries fell short of analyst estimates.

items.headline: Technology and Communication Services Led Weekly Gains, Energy Lagged
items.summary: For the week ending April 2, the Technology sector was the best performer, gaining 4.61%, followed by Communication Services, up 4.36%. In contrast, the Energy sector was the worst performer, declining 5.1%, while Consumer Defensives saw a modest rise of 0.72%. There is an observable rotation in market leadership, with investors favoring growth and momentum assets over energy and yield, and a shift away from the 'Magnificent Seven' towards a broader range of S&P 500 stocks year-to-date.

items.headline: Strong March Jobs Report Reinforces Fed 'Higher for Longer' Stance; Consumer Sentiment Slumps
items.summary: The U.S. labor market report for March, released on Friday, indicated a robust addition of 178,000 jobs and a decrease in the unemployment rate to 4.3%. This data largely supports expectations that the Federal Reserve will maintain its federal funds rate, with a 97.7% probability of no change at the upcoming April 2026 FOMC meeting. However, consumer sentiment has hit a concerning 56.6 as of April 3, reflecting distress amid geopolitical tensions and rising energy costs, with national average gasoline prices surging.

items.headline: Middle East Tensions Drive Oil Prices Higher, Fueling Inflation Fears
items.summary: Ongoing geopolitical tensions in the Middle East continue to significantly impact global markets. WTI crude oil futures briefly soared above $112 per barrel on April 3, contributing to rising energy bills and broader inflation concerns. This situation, including disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz, is expected to continue affecting commodity prices and supply chains, with potential price shocks for the U.S. market, particularly in California.

items.headline: Major Earnings Reports Loom in Mid-April
items.summary: While corporate earnings were subdued on April 4 due to the holiday, major companies are slated to report in mid-to-late April. Delta Air Lines ($DAL) is expected to release its Q1 results on April 8, with an anticipated EPS of $0.64. Amazon ($AMZN), Meta Platforms ($META), and Microsoft ($MSFT) are projected to announce their next earnings around April 28-29. Other notable reports are expected from Fastenal, Interactive Brokers Group, Goldman Sachs Group, BlackRock, Citigroup, Johnson & Johnson, JPMorgan Chase & Co., Wells Fargo & Company, and ASML starting April 13.

items.headline: AI Continues to Reshape Sector Fortunes: AMD Gains, Software Stocks Hit
items.summary: Artificial Intelligence remains a dominant theme, driving significant movements across sectors. Advanced Micro Devices ($AMD) bucked the broader market sell-off in March with a 2.5% gain, anticipating strong performance in 2026 fueled by AI chip shipments. Conversely, software stocks have been particularly hard-hit in Q1 2026 due to growing fears of AI disruption to existing business models.
